items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement budget plans
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's planned infrastructure and capital projects for the upcoming fiscal year. Residents can expect to learn what physical improvements and investments the city is prioritizing.

items.headline: City Council Considers Changes to Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance to amend its residential tenant protections program. This could affect rights and responsibilities for both renters and landlords in the city.

items.headline: City releases youth services plan and summer safety program updates
items.summary: The City Council reviewed status reports on children and youth services, including the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services programs. These reports track how the city is investing in youth development and safety initiatives across San José.

items.headline: Downtown building eligible for extra square footage via historic upgrade
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being considered to upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Figure AI expands San José office footprint
items.summary: The AI robotics company Figure AI is investing $4.1 million in a second-floor office tenant improvement at 4000 N 1st Street, reflecting continued growth of AI/robotics companies in the region.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road area
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties on San Antonio Road into an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a zoning change for the area.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 has been cancelled, pushing the next hearing to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will not be heard on the original date and will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is set.

items.headline: Major tech tenant improvement at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4 million commercial buildout is underway at 1768 Automation Parkway, indicating significant new tenant activity in San José's growing tech corridor. This is the largest single project in this permit batch and suggests major employer expansion or relocation to the area.
